title: Handwriting & Transcription
objective: Students will explore handwriting & transcription concepts including Writing digits 0-9, Forming Capital Letters, Letter Formation Families.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Writing digits 0-9!
Let's practice together! Write all digits 0-9 clearly with correct starting points
When you writes numbers like 3, 5, and 7, are they formed the right way and clearly readable — not reversed or written in an unusual direction?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Writing digits 0-9: Write all digits 0-9 clearly with correct starting points Form digits the appropriate size relative to letter height
Have you ever thought about Forming Capital Letters? Let's find out together!
Form capital letters correctly; print many upper- and lowercase letters legibly
Let's practice together! Write all 26 capital letters with correct formation
Can you write all the capital letters clearly and legibly so that someone reading their work can easily tell which letter is which?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Forming Capital Letters. Write all 26 capital letters with correct formation Distinguish between uppercase and lowercase forms in own writing
Today we're going to learn about Letter Formation Families. Get ready for an adventure!
Understand which letters belong to which handwriting families based on similar formation patterns and practise letters in groups
Let me show you something cool. Group letters by similar strokes (e.g. c, a, d, g, o family — all start with a curve)
Does you understand that certain letters are formed in similar ways — for example, "c," "a," "d," and "g" all start with the same curved stroke — and practise those families together?
Great job! Today we learned about Letter Formation Families. Group letters by similar strokes (e.g. c, a, d, g, o family — all start with a curve) Explain why certain letters are practised together
